zh.lifeSummary: William ap Thomas  "Y Marchog glas o Went"於1377年之前出生在威爾士蒙姆爾拉格藍。他於1392年與Lady Maud Morley Of Ragland Castle在英國威爾斯蒙姆爾拉格藍結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1446年在英國米德萊可倫敦，埋葬在威爾士蒙姆爾阿加文尼。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: William ap Thomas  "Y Marchog glas o Went"于1377年之前出生在威尔士蒙姆爾拉格藍。他于1392年与Lady Maud Morley Of Ragland Castle在英国威尔斯蒙姆爾拉格藍结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1446年在英国米德萊可倫敦，埋葬在威尔士蒙姆爾阿加文尼。

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

nameMeanings.name: glas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: o
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

variants.name: Oh
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: Went"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, in origin a local name from the English county. This is probably named with a Celtic word meaning ‘border’. Use as a given name is of recent origin, but it is now quite popular. It may in part be seen as a short form of   Kenton .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: William ap Thomas  "Y Marchog glas o Went" was born before 1377, in Raglan, Monmouthshire, Wales. He married Lady Maud Morley Of Ragland Castle in 1392, in Raglan, Monmouthshire, Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1446, in London, Middlesex, England, and was buried in Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Wales.

fr.lifeSummary: William ap Thomas  "Y Marchog glas o Went" est né avant 1377, à Raglan, Monmouthshire, Galles. Il a épousé Lady Maud Morley Of Ragland Castle en 1392, à Raglan, Monmouthshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1446, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre, et a été enterré à Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Galles.

ja.lifeSummary: William ap Thomas  "Y Marchog glas o Went"は1377年以前にウェールズモンモウスシュアイアラグランで生まれた。1392年にイギリスウェールズモンモウスシュアイアラグランでLady Maud Morley Of Ragland Castleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1446年にイギリスミッドレセックスロンドンで死去し，ウェールズモンモウスシュアイアアバーガベニーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William ap Thomas  "Y Marchog glas o Went"(은)는 1377년 이전 웨일스 몬모우스실르 라글런에서 태어났다. 그는 1392년 영국 웨일스 몬모우스실르 라글런에서 Lady Maud Morley Of Ragland Castle(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1446년 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 사망했으며 웨일스 몬모우스실르 압르가븐이에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: William ap Thomas  "Y Marchog glas o Went" родился перед 1377 г. в местности раглан, монмоутшир, Уэльс. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Maud Morley Of Ragland Castle в 1392 г. в местности раглан, монмоутшир, Уэльс, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1446 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия и был похоронен в местности абиргавенни, монмоутшир, Уэльс.
